examples.title: Process Creation Service
examples.description: Demonstrates how OS creates a new process using system calls
examples.code:
FUNCTION CreateProcess(programName, priority)
BEGIN
  // Allocate Process Control Block (PCB)
  newProcess = ALLOCATE(PCB)
  
  // Initialize PCB
  newProcess.processID = GenerateUniqueID()
  newProcess.state = NEW
  newProcess.programName = programName
  newProcess.priority = priority
  newProcess.programCounter = 0
  newProcess.registers = InitializeRegisters()
  newProcess.memoryList = EMPTY
  newProcess.openFiles = EMPTY
  newProcess.parentID = CurrentProcessID()
  
  // Load program into memory
  newProcess.memoryList = LoadProgram(programName)
  
  // Add to ready queue
  AddToReadyQueue(newProcess)
  newProcess.state = READY
  
  RETURN newProcess.processID
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how an OS creates a new process by allocating a Process Control Block (PCB), initializing its attributes, loading the program into memory, and adding it to the ready queue for scheduling.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is memory loading time
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is memory required for PCB and program

examples.title: Simple Memory Allocation Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates basic memory allocation and deallocation concepts
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates first-fit memory allocation where the OS finds the first available memory block large enough for a process. It manages free and allocated memory blocks, simulating how operating systems handle memory allocation and deallocation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for allocation search, O(n log n) for deallocation due to sorting
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of memory blocks

examples.title: File Write Operation Service
examples.description: Demonstrates file management service using system calls
examples.explanation: This C code simulates the file management service showing how the OS creates files using File Control Blocks (FCB), writes data to files with permission checking, and deletes files from the system.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for create and write, O(n) for delete/search
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) per file for FCB, O(size) for file data buffer

examples.title: I/O Device Management Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates device driver and buffer management concepts
examples.explanation: This Java code demonstrates I/O Device Management showing device driver simulation, request queuing (spooling), buffer management, and how the OS handles multiple I/O requests efficiently.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for n requests in queue, each processed sequentially
examples.spaceComplexity: O(BUFFER_SIZE) for buffer + O(n) for request queue
